sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Elche have shown decent form in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, with a knack for late goals as seen in recent games. Athletic Club, however, have been inconsistent, managing 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses, struggling defensively with no clean sheets in their last 9 matches. This suggests Elche might have a slight edge going into the match at home.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, results have been split with Elche winning 1-0 away in May 2023 and Athletic Club dominating 4-1 at Elche in September 2022. Matches between these teams often see low-scoring outcomes, with under 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 meetings. This history points to a potentially tight contest.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Elche, sitting 8th with 13 points, boast a strong defense (2nd least goals conceded) and high possession (3rd in the league at 57.5%), though they struggle to create corners (18th). Athletic Club, 9th with 13 points, have a balanced but underwhelming attack and defense, often conceding first (7 of 8 games). Elche's defensive solidity could be key.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Elche's André Silva has been in fine form, scoring 4 goals in his last 5 matches with a 7.22 Sofascore rating, posing a threat to Athletic's shaky defense. For Athletic Club, Álex Berenguer stands out in head-to-heads with a 7.56 average rating and 2 goals against Elche, making him a potential game-changer.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (40% home win, 25% draw, 35% away win) and Elche’s home advantage coupled with Athletic’s defensive woes, a narrow victory for Elche seems plausible. Expect a low-scoring game based on head-to-head trends and both teams’ recent form. Cards are likely, with Elche seeing over 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 matches.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Elche and Athletic Club at Estadio Martínez Valero ended in a goalless draw. Elche dominated possession and showed more intent in the final third, creating several opportunities to test the opposition defense. Athletic Club, on the other hand, struggled to impose themselves offensively, often resorting to a more defensive posture with limited forays into dangerous areas.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Elche has been the more assertive side in the opening 45 minutes, controlling the ball for the majority of the time. Their frequent entries into the final third and penalty area indicate a clear attacking intent. Players like Rafa Mir have been active in testing the Athletic Club defense, managing shots on target. However, despite their dominance, Elche has yet to convert their pressure into goals, which will be a key focus in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: Athletic Club has had a relatively quiet first half, with significantly less possession and fewer entries into dangerous zones compared to Elche. Their defensive structure has held firm so far, preventing Elche from capitalizing on their dominance. However, their lack of shots on target suggests a need for more creativity and urgency in attack if they are to turn the game in their favor.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Throughout the first half, Elche consistently maintained attacking momentum with several sustained spells of pressure, particularly in the middle and latter stages of the half. They showcased a clear intent to push forward and create scoring opportunities. Athletic Club had brief moments of momentum, especially early on and towards the end of the half, but these were far less frequent and lacked the same intensity as Elche's attacking phases.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Elche, the second half will likely focus on maintaining their high possession and translating their final third presence into goals. Players with high expected goal contributions could be pivotal. Athletic Club needs to improve their attacking output, potentially relying on counter-attacks or set-pieces to exploit any gaps in Elche's defense. Discipline will also be crucial for Elche, as they’ve picked up a couple of yellow cards already.
